label: 7326 Education and training programs on medical response to consequences of terrorist activities

children.heading: .—Education Program
children.label: a .—Education Program
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a program to develop and disseminate a series of model education and training programs on the medical responses to the consequences of terrorist activities.

children.heading: .—Implementing Official
children.label: b .—Implementing Official
children.text: The program shall be carried out through the Under Secretary for Health, in consultation with the Assistant Secretary of Veterans Affairs with responsibility for operations, preparedness, security, and law enforcement functions.
Recognition of chemical, biological, radiological, incendiary, or other explosive agents, weapons, or devices that may be used in terrorist activities.
Identification of the potential symptoms of exposure to those agents.
Understanding of the potential long-term health consequences, including psychological effects, resulting from exposure to those agents, weapons, or devices.
Emergency treatment for exposure to those agents, weapons, or devices.
An appropriate course of followup treatment, supportive care, and referral.
Actions that can be taken while providing care for exposure to those agents, weapons, or devices to protect against contamination, injury, or other hazards from such exposure.
Information on how to seek consultative support and to report suspected or actual use of those agents.

children.heading: .—Potential Trainees
children.label: d .—Potential Trainees
children.text: In designing the education and training programs under this section, the Secretary shall ensure that different programs are designed for health-care professionals in Department medical centers. The programs shall be designed to be disseminated to health professions students, graduate health and medical education trainees, and health practitioners in a variety of fields.

children.heading: .—Consultation
children.label: e .—Consultation
children.text: In establishing education and training programs under this section, the Secretary shall consult with appropriate representatives of accrediting, certifying, and coordinating organizations in the field of health professions education.
